name: Units
comment:
This module provides functionalities for parsing and displaying SI units
(and some other daily-use units).
This module has 4 groups of functionalities that is for

1.  creating `Unit` values
2.  parsing String into `Unit`
3.  displaying Unit as String in various form.
4.  simple arithmetics of `Quantity = (Float, Unit)`.


# Types

@docs Prefix, UnitSymbol, Unit, Error


## Defining New Unit Symbol

@docs createUnitSymbol


## Creation of Unit

@docs emptyUnit, u, pu, fromList, times, per, toThe, square, cubic, inverse, paren, simplifyUnitExpression


# Parsing

Unit parsing with this module proceeds in following pathway.

  - Defining set of units used in parser.
      - To avoid unit name collision, this process goes through `ValidatedUnitSymbols` type,
        whose creation may fail with name-conflicting units.
  - Converting `ValidatedUnitSymbols` into parser by `toParser` function.
      - This should be done only once at initialization process,
        because growing `ValidatedUnitSymbols` and creating parser is relatively heavy operation.
  - Using that parser with `parse` function.


## ValidatedUnitSymbols

You can:

  - use the default set of unit symbols (`defaultUnitSymbols`) provided by this module,
  - define new unit symbols as needed with `createUnitSymbol` and add them to the default set using `addUnitSymbols`,
  - or build your own set of unit symbols from scratch by folding `addUnitSymbols` over a list, starting from `emptyUnitSymbols`.

Some units (e.g. minute, inch, year, atm) are defined as **non-prefixable units** to avoid ambiguity with SI prefixes.

@docs ValidatedUnitSymbols, emptyUnitSymbols, defaultUnitSymbols, addUnitSymbols


## Getting and Using Parser

@docs toParser, failureParser, parse, Parser, DeadEnd


# Display

@docs toString


## Display Settings

@docs DisplayOption, defaultDisplayOption, defaultDisplayOptionForTex, inAscii, inTex, inUnicode, setSeparator, setSlash, setHeadingSlash, setParen, setEmptyUnitString, displayTopNumeratorOne, deleteTopNumeratorOne, useTopLevelTexFrac, doNotUseTopLevelTexFrac, useCustomSpacing, doNotUseCustomSpacing


# Arithmetics


## Note on unit growth

Units are combined syntactically when multiplying or dividing quantities.
This means repeated multiplications or divisions may result in large unit expressions
(e.g. `m * s * s`).

This is by design.

If you want to keep unit expressions compact or convert them into a specific unit,
call `convertInto` or `normalize` explicitly at appropriate points in your calculation.

@docs hasSameDimension, Quantity, convertInto, normalize, add, subtractBy, multiplyBy, divideBy, applySequentially


# Predefined Unit Symbols

The following sections list predefined unit symbols and prefixes.
They are primarily reference material and are not required
for understanding the core API.

Some units (e.g. minute, inch, year, atm) are defined as **non-prefixable units** to avoid ambiguity with SI prefixes.

@docs meter, gram, second, kelvin, ampere, mole, candela, ton, minute, hour, day, year, newton, pascal, atmosphere, millimeterOfMercury, torr, bar, joule, watt, degreeCelsius, hertz, coulomb, volt, ohm, siemens, farad, weber, tesla, henry, lumen, lux, becquerel, gray, sievert, liter, degree, radian, steradian, inch, foot, yard, pound


# Prefix

@docs quecto, ronto, yocto, zepto, atto, femto, pico, nano, micro, milli, centi, deci, noPrefix, deca, hecto, kilo, mega, giga, tera, peta, exa, zetta, yotta, quetta

unions.name: DisplayOption
unions.comment:
Type that represents configurations for displaying units as strings.
This includes options about:

  - output format
      - for now, 3 options are available.
          - ascii characters like `um s^-1` (This is default value)
          - unicode characters like `μm s⁻¹`
          - tex string like `{\\rm{{\\mu}{m}}\\,/\\,{s^3}}`.
  - unit separator in arbitrary string ( default value is " ")
  - how dimensionless unit should be displayed ( default value is "[-]")
  - In which way `one |> per (u second)` should be expressed, `1 / s` or `/ s`.
  - (for tex user) the outermost slash symbol could be expressed in `\\frac{<numerator>}{<denominator>}` style.

unions.name: Error
unions.comment:
Represents all recoverable errors that may occur during
unit parsing, conversion, or arithmetic operations.

unions.name: Prefix
unions.comment: Type representing SI prefix ("kilo" in "kilometer" etc.)

unions.name: UnitSymbol
unions.comment: Type that represents unit before prefixed, like "meter" in "millimeter".

unions.name: ValidatedUnitSymbols
unions.comment:
Represents unit symbols usables in parsing purpose,
in which no name collision could occurs.
This type is exposed so that users can control
which unit symbols are accepted by the parser.

aliases.name: Quantity
aliases.comment: Representing phisical quantity that is unit multiplied by numerical coefficient.

values.name: add
values.comment:
Adds two quantities.
Fails if unit for either of addend contains inconvertible unit like ℃,
or two addeds have different dimensions.

values.name: addUnitSymbols
values.comment:
The only way to add a user-defined unit symbol for parsing purpose.

This function fails if the new unit symbol conflicts with existing
symbols or their prefixed forms.

        eV : UnitSymbol
        eV =
            createUnitSymbol
                { display = "eV"
                , parserInput = ["eV"]
                , baseSIExpression =
                    { meterExponent = 2
                    , kilogramExponent = 1
                    , secondExponent = -2
                    , kelvinExponent = 0
                    , ampereExponent = 0
                    , moleExponent = 0
                    , candelaExponent = 0
                    , numericalValue =
                        Ok (1.602176634e-19)
                    }
                , allowPrefix = True
                }

        parser : Parser
        parser =
             defaultUnitSymbols
                |> addUnitSymbols [eV]
                |> Result.map toParser
                |> Result.withDefault failureParser

        parsedUnit : Unit
        parsedUnit =
            case parse parser "keV" of
                Ok unit ->
                    unit
                Err _ ->
                    emptyUnit


        (200, parsedUnit)
            |> convertInto (u eV)
            --> Ok (toFloat (round 200000), u eV)
values.type: List.List Units.UnitSymbol -> Units.ValidatedUnitSymbols -> Result.Result Units.Error Units.ValidatedUnitSymbols

values.name: ampere
values.comment: Unit for electric current denoted as `A`.

values.name: applySequentially
values.comment: Applies transformations sequentially, stopping at the first error.
values.type: List.List (Units.Quantity -> Result.Result Units.Error Units.Quantity) -> Units.Quantity -> Result.Result Units.Error Units.Quantity

values.name: atmosphere
values.comment: Unit for pressure denoted as `atm`. Equals to `1.01325×10^5 Pa`.

values.name: atto
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(-18)

values.name: bar
values.comment: Unit for pressure denoted as `bar`. Equals to `10^5 Pa`.

values.name: becquerel
values.comment: Unit for radio activity denoted as `Bq`. Equals to `1 / s`.

values.name: candela
values.comment: Unit for luminous intensity denoted as `cd`.

values.name: centi
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(-2)

values.name: convertInto
values.comment:
Converts expression of physical quantity with unit into another unit.
This fails when:

  - The targeted unit and start unit has different dimensions.

  - Units like `℃`, `Sv` or `year` is involved.

```
    (3, cubic (u meter))
        |> convertInto (u liter)
        |> Result.map
            (\(numericalValue, unit) ->
                    String.fromInt (round numericalValue)
                    ++ " "
                    ++ toString defaultDisplayOption unit
            )
        --> Ok "3000 L"
```

values.name: coulomb
values.comment: Unit for electricity denoted as `C`. Equals to `A・s`.

values.name: createUnitSymbol
values.comment:
Creates a `UnitSymbol`, which represents an unprefixed unit
such as "meter" in "millimeter".

This function is mainly intended for defining custom unit symbols
that can later be added to `ValidatedUnitSymbols` for parsing.
values.type: { parserInput : List.List String.String, display : String.String, baseSIExpression : { meterExponent : Basics.Int, kilogramExponent : Basics.Int, secondExponent : Basics.Int, kelvinExponent : Basics.Int, ampereExponent : Basics.Int, moleExponent : Basics.Int, candelaExponent : Basics.Int, numericalValue : Result.Result String.String Basics.Float }, allowPrefix : Basics.Bool } -> Units.UnitSymbol

values.name: cubic
values.comment:
Syntax suger for `toThe 3`.

    cubic (pu deci meter)
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "dm^3"

values.name: day
values.comment: Unit for time denoted as `day`. Equals to `86400 s`.

values.name: deca
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(1)

values.name: deci
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(-1)

values.name: defaultDisplayOption
values.comment: default values for `DisplayOption`

values.name: defaultDisplayOptionForTex
values.comment: default values for `DisplayOption` tuned for TeX output.

values.name: defaultUnitSymbols
values.comment:
Represents `ValidatedUnitSymbols` value
with SI standarized units and some other daily use unit.

values.name: degree
values.comment: Unit for angle denoted as `°`. Equals to `(π/180) rad`.

values.name: degreeCelsius
values.comment:
Unit for temperature denoted as `℃`. A temperature of `t ℃` is equal to `(t + 273.15) K`.
In this package, conversion involving this unit is not supported.

values.name: deleteTopNumeratorOne
values.comment: Display like `/ s`.

values.name: displayTopNumeratorOne
values.comment: Display like `1 / s`.

values.name: divideBy
values.comment:
Divides one quantity by another.
Fails if unit for either of two contains inconvertible unit like ℃.

values.name: doNotUseCustomSpacing
values.comment: cancels `useCustomSpacing`.

values.name: doNotUseTopLevelTexFrac
values.comment: Display like `\\left({\\rm{mol}}\\,/\\,{\\rm{L}}\\right)\\,/\\,{\\rm{s}}`

values.name: emptyUnit
values.comment: Unit with no dimension. Equals to 1.

values.name: emptyUnitSymbols
values.comment: Represents vacant `ValidatedUnitSymbols` value

values.name: exa
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(18)

values.name: failureParser
values.comment:
This parser always fails.
Intended to be used as a fallback when `addUnitSymbols` fails,
which indicates a configuration error rather than a runtime parsing error.

values.name: farad
values.comment: Unit for capacity denoted as `F`. Equals to `C / V`.

values.name: femto
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(-15)

values.name: foot
values.comment: Unit for length denoted as `ft`.

values.name: fromList
values.comment:
Creates `Unit` value from `List ( Prefix, UnitSymbol, Int )`,
where the `Int` value represents exponent.
The order in the list is preserved and used as the displayed order.
In complex case where you want to use `/` or parened exponentiation
like `kg (m / s)^2`, other functions may be more suited.

    fromList [(centi, meter, 1), (noPrefix, second, -1)]
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "cm s^-1"

values.name: giga
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(9)

values.name: gram
values.comment: Unit for mass denoted as `g`.

values.name: gray
values.comment: Unit for absorbed dose denoted as `Gy`. Equals to `J / kg`.

values.name: hasSameDimension
values.comment:
Checks if two arguments have the same dimension.

    u liter |> hasSameDimension (cubic (u meter))
    --> True

    u liter |> hasSameDimension (square (u meter))
    --> False

values.name: hecto
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(2)

values.name: henry
values.comment: Unit for self or mutual inductance denoted as `H`. Equals to `V / (A s^-1)`.

values.name: hertz
values.comment: Unit for frequency denoted as `Hz`. Equals to s^-1

values.name: hour
values.comment: Unit for time denoted as `h`. Equals to `3600 s`.

values.name: inAscii
values.comment: Change the output format into ascii string

values.name: inTex
values.comment: Change the output format into tex string

values.name: inUnicode
values.comment: Change the output format into unicode string

values.name: inch
values.comment: Unit for length denoted as `in`.

values.name: inverse
values.comment:
Syntax suger for `toThe -1`.
Note that this is different from `power` at display.

    u mole |> times (inverse (u liter)) |> per (u second)
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "mol L^-1 / s"

values.name: joule
values.comment: Unit for work and energy denoted as `J`. Equals to `N・m`.

values.name: kelvin
values.comment: Unit for absolute temperature denoted as `K`.

values.name: kilo
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(3)

values.name: liter
values.comment: Unit for volume denoted as `L`. Equals to `10^(-3) m^3`.

values.name: lumen
values.comment: Unit for luminous flux denoted as `Lm`. Equals to `cd・sr`.

values.name: lux
values.comment: Unit for illuminance denoted as `Lx`. Equals to `Lm / m^2`.

values.name: mega
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(6)

values.name: meter
values.comment: Unit for length denoted as `m`.

values.name: micro
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(-6)

values.name: milli
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(-3)

values.name: millimeterOfMercury
values.comment: Unit for pressure denoted as `mmHg`. Equals to `1/760 atm`.

values.name: minute
values.comment: Unit for time denoted as `min`. Equals to `60 s`.

values.name: mole
values.comment: Unit for amount of substance denoted as `mol`.

values.name: multiplyBy
values.comment:
Multiply one quantity by another.
Fails if unit for either of two contains inconvertible unit like ℃.

values.name: nano
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(-9)

values.name: newton
values.comment: Unit for force denoted as `N`. Equals to `kg m s^-2`.

values.name: noPrefix
values.comment: Prefix type value that shows nothing is prefixed.

values.name: normalize
values.comment:
Converts quantity into normalized unit in the form of `m^a kg^b s^c K^d A^e mol^f cd^g`,
exponent 1 and unit with 0 exponent being omitted.
The numerical value is also converted accordingly.

values.name: ohm
values.comment: Unit for resistance denoted as `Ω`. Equals to `V / A`.

values.name: paren
values.comment: Adds paren to unit. This affects displayed string.

values.name: parse
values.comment: Runs the parser onto given string.

values.name: pascal
values.comment: Unit for pressure denoted as `Pa`. Equals to `N m^-2`.

values.name: per
values.comment:
Represents division with slash (not with negative exponent).
Intended to be used with `|>` operator.
Note that `|>` is left associative.

    u joule |> per (u kelvin) |> times (u gram)
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "(J / K) g"

    u joule |> per ((u kelvin) |> times (u gram))
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "J / (K g)"

    u joule |> per (u kelvin) |> per (u gram)
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "(J / K) / g"

    u joule |> times (inverse (u kelvin)) |> per (u gram)
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "J K^-1 / g"

values.name: peta
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(15)

values.name: pico
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(-12)

values.name: pound
values.comment: Unit for mass denoted as `lb`.

values.name: pu
values.comment:
Creates `Unit` value from `Prefix` and `UnitSymbol`.

    pu kilo pascal
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "kPa"

values.name: quecto
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(-30)

values.name: quetta
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(30)

values.name: radian
values.comment: Unit for angle denoted as `rad`.

values.name: ronto
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(-27)

values.name: second
values.comment: Unit for time denoted as `s`.

values.name: setEmptyUnitString
values.comment: Change the expression of empty unit(`1`) into arbitrary string.

values.name: setHeadingSlash
values.comment: Change the heading slash (like "/ " in "6.02×10²³ / mol") into arbitrary string.

values.name: setParen
values.comment: Change the paren into arbitrary string.
values.type: { open : String.String, close : String.String } -> Units.DisplayOption -> Units.DisplayOption

values.name: setSeparator
values.comment: Change the unit separator into arbitrary string.

values.name: setSlash
values.comment: Change the slash into arbitrary string.

values.name: siemens
values.comment: Unit for conductivity denoted as `S`. Equals to `A / V`.

values.name: sievert
values.comment:
Unit for exposure dose denoted as `Sv`. The dimension equals to `J / kg`,
but with different weight coefficient applied to different radio beam kind.

values.name: simplifyUnitExpression
values.comment:
Simplifies unit expressions by applying semantic normalization, such as:

  - `(s^-1)^2` is converted into `s^-2`.
  - `m / 1`, `m 1` or `m^1` are all converted into `m`.

```
    litterInSI : Unit
    litterInSI = cubic (pu deci meter)

    square litterInSI
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "(dm^3)^2"

    square litterInSI
    |> simplifyUnitExpression
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "dm^6"
```

values.name: square
values.comment:
Syntax suger for `toThe 2`.

    square (u meter)
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "m^2"

values.name: steradian
values.comment: Unit for stereo angle denoted as `sr`.

values.name: subtractBy
values.comment:
Subtracts one quantity from another.
Fails if unit for either subtrahend or minuend contains inconvertible unit like ℃,
or subtrahend and minuend have different dimensions.

values.name: tera
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(12)

values.name: tesla
values.comment: Unit for magnetic flux density denoted as `T`. Equals to `Wb / m^2`.

values.name: times
values.comment:
Represents multiplication of units.

    u meter |> times (inverse (u second))
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "m s^-1"

values.name: toParser
values.comment:
Converts a set of unit symbols into a `Parser`.

If you want to build a parser that accepts **only SI base units**
(with or without prefixes), you can write:

    baseUnitsParser : Parser
    baseUnitsParser =
        emptyUnitSymbols
            |> addUnitSymbols
                [ meter, gram, second, kelvin, ampere, mole, candela ]
            |> Result.map toParser
            |> Result.withDefault failureParser

This gives you a parser that rejects any non-SI or user-defined unit symbols.

    parse baseUnitsParser "cm s^-1"
        |> Result.map (toString defaultDisplayOption)
        --> Ok "cm s^-1"

    parse baseUnitsParser "km / h"
        |> Result.map (toString defaultDisplayOption)
        |> Result.withDefault "failed to parse!"
        --> "failed to parse!"

values.name: toString
values.comment: Converts unit into string with specified display options.

values.name: toThe
values.comment:
Represents exponentiation of units.
Intended to be used with `|>` operator.
Note that `|>` is left associative.

    u mole |> per (pu deci meter |> toThe 3) |> toThe 2
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "(mol / dm^3)^2"

values.name: ton
values.comment: Unit for mass denoted as `ton`. Equals to `10^6 kg`.

values.name: torr
values.comment: Unit for pressure denoted as `torr`. Equals to `mmHg`.

values.name: u
values.comment:
Creates `Unit` value from `UnitSymbol` with no prefix added.

    u pascal
    |> toString defaultDisplayOption
    --> "Pa"

values.name: useCustomSpacing
values.comment: display like "K /(mol / kg)". Note that space between "/" and "(" are removed.

values.name: useTopLevelTexFrac
values.comment:
Display like `\\frac{{\\rm{mol}}\\,/\\,{\\rm{L}}}{{\\rm{s}}}`.
This enforces `displayTopNumeratorOne` to be applied.

values.name: volt
values.comment: Unit for voltage denoted as `V`. Equals to `J / C`.

values.name: watt
values.comment: Unit for power denoted as `W`. Equals to `J / s`.

values.name: weber
values.comment: Unit for magnetic flux denoted as `Wb`. Equals to `J / A`.

values.name: yard
values.comment: Unit for length denoted as `yd`.

values.name: year
values.comment:
Unit for time denoted as `year`. The exact definition may vary depending on context.
In this package, conversion involving this unit is not supported.

values.name: yocto
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(-24)

values.name: yotta
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(24)

values.name: zepto
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(-21)

values.name: zetta
values.comment: Prefix that represents 10^(21)
